title: Oracle Identity Manager & Web Services Manager Emergency Out-of-Band RCE CVE-2026-21992 (CVSS 9.8): Missing Authentication in REST WebServices Enables Unauthenticated Full System Compromise — Second Emergency Oracle Alert in Product History

sources.name: The Hacker News - Oracle Patches Critical CVE-2026-21992
Tenable Blog - CVE-2026-21992: Critical Out-of-Band Oracle Identity Manager RCE
Help Net Security - Oracle Emergency Fix CVE-2026-21992
BleepingComputer - Oracle Pushes Emergency Fix for Critical Identity Manager RCE Flaw
Security Affairs - Oracle Fixes Critical RCE Flaw CVE-2026-21992 in Identity Manager
SecurityWeek - Oracle Releases Emergency Patch for Critical Identity Manager Vulnerability
Dark Reading - Patch Oracle Fusion Middleware RCE Flaw
Security Boulevard - CVE-2026-21992 Out-of-Band Oracle Identity Manager RCE
Arctic Wolf Threat Intelligence - CVE-2026-21992 Assessment
Sophos Security Blog - Oracle Vulnerability CVE-2026-21992 Impacts Core Products
CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog (CVE-2025-61757 - related)

timeline.event: Related vulnerability CVE-2025-61757 (authentication bypass in Oracle Identity Manager REST WebServices) patched in October 2025 Critical Patch Update
CVE-2025-61757 added to CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog after active exploitation confirmed in the wild
Oracle Security Alert for CVE-2026-21992 initially released — only the second out-of-band emergency alert in Oracle Identity Manager product history
Oracle Security Alert finalized and out-of-band patch distributed for both Oracle Identity Manager and Oracle Web Services Manager (versions 12.2.1.4.0 and 14.1.2.1.0)
Public disclosure begins: The Hacker News, BleepingComputer, Security Affairs report on emergency alert
Security researchers confirm CVSS 9.8 rating; analysis notes similarity to prior CVE-2025-61757 suggesting either a patch bypass or parallel vulnerability in the same REST WebServices component
Help Net Security and other outlets report on broader impact; organizations begin emergency patching
No public PoC exploit available; no confirmed in-the-wild exploitation reported for CVE-2026-21992 specifically; patching recommended immediately given CVE-2025-61757 exploitation precedent

simple_summary.what_happened: Oracle issued a rare emergency security alert — only the second such out-of-band patch in Oracle Identity Manager's history — for a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-21992) that allows attackers to remotely execute code on Oracle Identity Manager and Oracle Web Services Manager servers without any authentication. The flaw exists in the REST WebServices component, where critical functions can be accessed without requiring a login. Oracle Identity Manager is the software many large enterprises use to control who has access to what systems — managing user accounts, permissions, and authentication across the organization. Compromising it means an attacker can potentially manipulate all identity and access controls. The vulnerability is closely related to a prior flaw (CVE-2025-61757) that was already actively exploited and added to CISA's KEV list in November 2025.
simple_summary.who_is_affected: Organizations running Oracle Identity Manager or Oracle Web Services Manager versions 12.2.1.4.0 or 14.1.2.1.0. These products are commonly deployed at large enterprises, government agencies, financial institutions, and healthcare organizations that use Oracle Fusion Middleware as part of their identity and access management (IAM) infrastructure. Oracle issues security patches through quarterly Critical Patch Updates, making out-of-band alerts extremely rare and a strong signal of severity.
simple_summary.what_attackers_do: Step 1: Attacker discovers an Oracle Identity Manager or Web Services Manager instance accessible over the network (often via internet-facing enterprise portals). Step 2: Attacker sends unauthenticated HTTP requests directly to the REST WebServices API endpoint, bypassing all authentication controls due to missing authentication enforcement (CWE-306). Step 3: Without needing any credentials, attacker executes arbitrary code on the Oracle Fusion Middleware server through the exposed REST API. Step 4: Attacker gains operating system-level access to the Identity Manager server. Step 5: With control of the Identity Manager, attacker can create new privileged accounts, grant themselves access to any system managed by Oracle IAM, harvest existing user credentials, and pivot laterally throughout the entire enterprise network.
what_you_should_do: Apply Oracle's out-of-band security alert patch for CVE-2026-21992 immediately — contact Oracle Support for the patch if not yet received
Restrict network access to Oracle Identity Manager and Web Services Manager REST API endpoints to authorized internal IP ranges only
Review Oracle Identity Manager audit logs for unauthorized API calls, especially from external IP addresses, since March 19, 2026
Check if you previously applied the patch for CVE-2025-61757 (October 2025 CPU) — if not, apply both patches urgently
Audit all privileged accounts in Oracle Identity Manager for unauthorized additions or modifications
simple_summary.why_this_is_serious: Oracle Identity Manager is the master keychain for enterprise access control — it manages who can log into what across the entire organization. An attacker who compromises it doesn't just breach one system; they get the keys to every door. Oracle's decision to issue only its second-ever out-of-band emergency alert for this product reflects how seriously they view the risk. The related CVE-2025-61757 in the same component was already exploited in the wild and added to CISA's KEV list, demonstrating that threat actors actively target Oracle IAM infrastructure. A successful compromise could enable attackers to silently add backdoor accounts, escalate privileges across the enterprise, and evade detection while operating as legitimate users.

tactics.description: Attackers exploit the missing authentication vulnerability in Oracle Identity Manager's REST WebServices API endpoint, which may be accessible over the network or internet in many enterprise deployments.
Remote code execution via the unauthenticated REST API allows attackers to run arbitrary commands on the Oracle Fusion Middleware server operating system.
With control of Oracle Identity Manager, attackers can create hidden privileged accounts, modify existing account permissions, and establish persistent access across all IAM-managed systems.
Compromising Oracle Identity Manager enables attackers to grant themselves or malicious accounts elevated privileges across any system that relies on Oracle IAM for authentication and authorization.
Attackers use Oracle Identity Manager to create or modify accounts with valid credentials across integrated enterprise systems, enabling legitimate-looking lateral movement throughout the network.

detection_guidance.summary: Focus on monitoring REST WebServices API access patterns for unauthenticated or anomalous requests. Oracle Identity Manager audit logs, network perimeter logs, and IAM event logs are primary detection surfaces. No public IOCs exist yet; behavioral and anomaly-based detection is essential.

log_sources.description: Monitor OIM application audit logs for REST API calls made without valid authentication tokens, especially to administrative endpoints (provisioning, role management, user creation)

log_sources.source: Oracle Access Manager / Fusion Middleware Logs
log_sources.description: Check Oracle Access Manager logs for authentication bypass events or REST WebServices access patterns that do not match normal user session flows
Monitor for HTTP/HTTPS connections to Oracle Identity Manager REST API ports from external or unexpected IP addresses
Monitor the OIM backend database for unexpected user creation, role assignment, or privilege escalation events that could indicate post-exploitation account manipulation

what_to_look_for.indicator: Unauthenticated requests to OIM REST WebServices endpoints
what_to_look_for.description: HTTP requests hitting OIM REST API paths without a valid session token or OAuth2 bearer token, particularly to /iam/governance/ or /sysadmin/ REST paths
what_to_look_for.log_field: request_path CONTAINS '/iam/governance/' AND auth_token IS NULL

what_to_look_for.description: Post-exploitation, attackers will create backdoor accounts in OIM. Monitor for new SYSTEM_ADMIN or privileged role assignments, especially outside change management windows
what_to_look_for.log_field: event_type='USER_CREATED' AND role CONTAINS 'ADMIN' AND source='REST_API'

what_to_look_for.indicator: REST API calls from external IP addresses
what_to_look_for.description: Oracle Identity Manager REST WebServices should only be accessed from internal network segments; any external internet IP making API calls is highly suspicious
what_to_look_for.log_field: source_ip NOT IN internal_ip_ranges AND request_path CONTAINS '/iam/'

what_to_look_for.indicator: High volume of REST API requests in short time window
what_to_look_for.description: Exploitation and post-exploitation reconnaissance may generate an unusual burst of REST API calls; baseline normal API call rates and alert on deviations

immediate_actions.action: Apply Oracle out-of-band security patch for CVE-2026-21992
immediate_actions.description: Obtain and apply the emergency out-of-band patch from Oracle Support immediately. This is the only complete remediation. Oracle issued this alert with the same urgency level as CVE-2017-10151 (the previous out-of-band Oracle IAM alert).

immediate_actions.action: Restrict network access to Oracle Identity Manager REST API
immediate_actions.description: Immediately restrict inbound connections to Oracle Identity Manager's application server ports (typically 14000, 14001, or 7001/7002) to authorized internal IP ranges using firewall ACLs. No external internet access should be permitted to these services.
immediate_actions.command:
# iptables example: block external access to OIM port
iptables -I INPUT -p tcp --dport 14000 -s 0.0.0.0/0 -j DROP
iptables -I INPUT -p tcp --dport 14000 -s 10.0.0.0/8 -j ACCEPT

immediate_actions.action: Verify October 2025 CPU patch for CVE-2025-61757 was applied
immediate_actions.description: CVE-2025-61757 (same component, same severity, actively exploited) should have been patched in October 2025. Confirm this patch is applied — if not, apply both the October 2025 CPU and the March 2026 out-of-band patch.

immediate_actions.action: Audit Oracle Identity Manager for unauthorized account changes
immediate_actions.description: Run an immediate audit of all accounts created, modified, or granted elevated privileges in Oracle Identity Manager since March 19, 2026. Investigate any unexpected changes, especially SYSTEM_ADMIN role assignments.

immediate_actions.action: Enable enhanced audit logging for REST WebServices
immediate_actions.description: If not already enabled, configure Oracle Identity Manager to log all REST WebServices API access with full authentication context. This ensures rapid detection of any exploitation attempts going forward.
